title: Pilea angulata
intro: Pilea angulata, also known as the aluminium plant, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Urticaceae, native to Southeast Asia and widely cultivated for its attractive, silver-dollar shaped leaves. The leaves of Pilea angulata have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pilea angulata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Pilea angulata is often made into tea, infusions, or extracts, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
taxonomy: Pilea angulata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae. This family is commonly known as the nettle family or figworts. The plant is part of the genus Pilea, which consists of various species of shrubs and herbs. Pilea angulata is native to Southeast Asia and is often cultivated as an ornamental plant. It is a member of the order Rosales, which includes other flowering plants such as roses and hawthorns.
uses: Pilea angulata, a versatile houseplant, has garnered attention for its potential medicinal uses. While research is still in its infancy, some studies suggest that extracts from Pilea angulata may possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties could potentially be used to develop new treatments for various health issues, including skin conditions and wounds.
